Meta.title: NanaiCare — Beauty & facial care in Amsterdam
Meta.description: A calm, professional studio in Amsterdam for facials, skin health guidance, and curated skincare—rooted in care.

Hero.title: Care that meets your skin where it is.
Hero.lead: NanaiCare is a boutique beauty studio for facials, restorative touch, and honest guidance—designed to feel calm, clear, and close.

Services.label: What we offer
Services.title: Treatments shaped around rhythm, not rush.
Services.subtitle: Every session is paced for listening: your lifestyle, your barrier, your goals.

panel.title: Studio rhythm
panel.body: Quiet music, warmed towels, and language you can understand—no gatekeeping, just care.

items.title: Nanai facial ritual
items.description: A slow, layered facial with gentle massage, tailored masks, and finishing care for luminous balance.

items.title: Facial contour massage
items.description: Light manual techniques to soften tension, support circulation, and leave the face feeling lifted and at ease.

items.title: Barrier reset treatment
items.description: For sensitized or over-stimulated skin: soothing steps that rebuild comfort and clarity over time.

items.title: Skin consultation
items.description: A guided conversation with a practical home routine—fewer products, better decisions.

Testimonials.label: Stories
Testimonials.title: Warm words from people who sit in our chair.

items.quote: I leave softer—inside and out. The space feels intentional, and the advice never feels like a sales pitch.
items.name: Maya R.

items.quote: Finally a place that explains what my skin needs without overwhelm. The massage portion is unreal.
items.name: Jonas V.

items.quote: Luxurious, but grounded. My rosacea-prone skin has never been this steady.
items.name: Elena S.

Wellness.label: Care notes
Wellness.title: Small recommendations with outsized kindness.
Wellness.subtitle: Short, evidence-minded tips you can actually keep—hydration, sleep, sun, and seasonal shifts.

cards.title: Morning light, gentler skin
cards.body: Ten minutes of daylight early supports rhythm—and often helps reactive skin stay calmer through the week.

cards.title: Less friction, more repair
cards.body: Press products in; avoid rubbing dry. Friction is a quiet source of redness many routines overlook.

cards.title: Seasonal barrier check
cards.body: When the canals bite, swap stripping cleansers for cream textures—barrier first, actives second.

Shop.label: Skincare
Shop.title: Curated products for home care.
Shop.subtitle: A tight edit of cleansers, serums, and barrier creams we trust—chosen for real Amsterdam weather and real lives.

Footer.tagline: NanaiCare — facial wellness & boutique beauty in Amsterdam.

meta.title: Project charter — NanaiCare
meta.description: Scope, roles, and agreed budget reference for the NanaiCare digital project (Sambalab · DGRcodex).

Charter.title: NanaiCare project charter
Charter.lead: Single-page agreement for scope boundaries, responsibilities, and the agreed payment structure. Figures are planning references; final amounts follow the issued invoice.
Charter.overviewP1: Align NanaiCare, Sambalab, and development on what is in and out of scope for the first web phase, and record the agreed budget split (50% + 50%).
Charter.overviewP2: Digital vision: mobile-first, warm and professional, EN / ES / NL, services, stories, wellness notes, and a clear path toward a future skincare shop.
Charter.partyClientBody: Final copy, tone, legal and pricing decisions, appointments, domain purchase, and agreed payments.
Charter.partyAgencyBody: Client liaison, prioritisation, and communication of deliverables.
Charter.partyDevBody: Next.js architecture, i18n, UI aligned with the brand system, deployment, and concise technical handover.
Charter.briefIntro: The literal brief document should be pasted below when you have it. Until then, this summary reflects what we are building together.
briefBullets: Beauty and facial wellness studio in Amsterdam, Netherlands.
Public site in English, Spanish, and Dutch.
Mobile-first experience: services, testimonials, wellness recommendations, and a skincare shop teaser.
Scalable foundation for later booking and e-commerce phases.
Charter.briefPlaceholder: When you receive the full text or PDF from the client, replace this block with the exact wording or attach it in docs/.
scopeIn: Next.js site with locale routing and shared brand UI.
Home narrative: hero, services, testimonials, wellness strip, shop teaser.
Deployment guidance (e.g. Vercel) and repository handover.
Charter.scopeOutTitle: Out of scope unless separately agreed
scopeOut: Full payment gateway, shipping, and operational e-commerce.
Native mobile apps, complex CRM, or 24/7 maintenance SLA.
Specialist legal review beyond baseline privacy copy placeholders.
Custom studio photography unless provided or sourced as agreed.
Charter.changeBody: Features not listed here, or integrations that require new vendors, are quoted as phase 2 or a written change request. Work that invalidates already approved deliverables may affect timeline and cost.
Charter.rowVat: VAT (21% on base — confirm on invoice)
Charter.budgetNote: Domain and VAT lines depend on registrar and tax treatment; use the issued invoice as the binding reference.
Charter.paymentIntro: Agreed structure: two halves of the indicative total. First half: 50%. Second half: 50% upon the agreed completion milestone.
Charter.weekGoal: Target: settle the outstanding first-half balance this week, as verbally agreed between the parties.
Charter.diagramCaption: Scope view: phase 1 vs optional later work.
Charter.diagramAlt: Flow: Phase 1 delivers Next site, brand UI, deploy; Phase 2 options include e-commerce, bookings, extra legal.
